what_it_does: Backend source-router for an interactive 'brain' assistant: it classifies a natural-language question by shape, picks one of three cognition sources ('fleet' for live fabric/session state, 'recall' for memory, 'model' for general chat), and composes a grounded answer using existing read paths and the suite's chat primitive, with fail-soft degrade to the model source.
observations: Routing is deterministic keyword-substring matching over two hint tuples (_FLEET_HINTS, _RECALL_HINTS), falling back to 'model'.
The 'fleet' source is read-only: it reports channel/session counts and recent traffic, and may emit a gated 'proposal' object, but never wakes or spawns sessions.
recent_channel_context loads channel events from the store, resolves message bodies, and returns the newest N posts as context.
_model_answer does not hardcode a brain model; it calls pick_ollama_model_for_context with a ~16K estimate and routes through OLLAMA_DIRECT.
When an 'aim' is provided, territory_prose is folded into the prompt so the answer can be grounded to the operator's current focus.
The 'recall' source is explicitly a declared seam/hook: v1 answers via the tool-grounded model and notes where recollection's backend should later compose.
Fail-soft is pervasive: unroutable questions go to 'model', source errors degrade to 'model' with a note, and model errors return an error dict rather than raising.
Answer extraction from suite.chat tries 'reply', then 'text', then 'answer'.
standouts: Fleet source stays read-only and surfaces actions as proposals, never executing them.
Brain model is resolved dynamically by context-size rule instead of a DEFAULT_BRAIN constant.
Recall source is not faked: it is a named seam left for recollection's backend to wire.
The module frames source selection as a discrete 'resolver axis' using a declared routing table.
conventions: Follows 'reuse-don't-parallel' by composing existing fold_channels, list_agent_sessions, and Suite.chat rather than building parallel readers.
Follows a 'floor' safety rule: read + propose, never dispatch/spawn.
Uses late/local imports inside functions to avoid circular import issues.
Uses private underscore-prefixed helpers for internal composition steps.
Heavy docstrings state architectural intent and anti-patterns avoided.
concerns: route_source uses simple substring matching and a hardcoded tie-break (fleet > recall > model), which can misclassify ambiguous questions.
_fleet_answer calls _model_answer before its definition in the file; this works only because the name is resolved at call time, not load time.
recent_channel_context loads all channel events since -1 and filters/slices in memory rather than querying a bounded window.
Broad 'except Exception' swallowing in ask and _fleet_answer hides failure details from callers.
The 'recall' source is currently just a model answer with a seam note, so recall questions do not actually use recollection search.
Message bodies in fleet answers are truncated to 300 characters with no indication of truncation.
notes: A safety-first brain backend that resolves questions to read-only sources and always degrades to a conversational model rather than crashing.
questions: What are the exact contracts of Suite.chat, Suite.list_agent_sessions, and the store's get_content / channel_events_since?
How is OLLAMA_DIRECT configured and what model names does pick_ollama_model_for_context return?
What does territory_prose produce, and how does the 'aim' address map to a real card or object?
What is the expected request/response shape of /api/brain/ask and how does the frontend render 'proposal' objects?
When and how will the richer 'recall' source be wired into _model_answer or ask?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements the stated backend half — deterministic source resolution, read-only fleet composition, model fallback, and an explicit recall seam.
intention_signals: Build a safe, explainable brain router that can answer about live fabric state and general questions without taking autonomous action, using existing primitives and dynamic model selection while leaving memory and execution to other lanes.
novelty: Treats the brain as a role resolved to a model via a context-size picker, uses a deterministic resolver-axis for source selection, and returns explicit gated-action proposals inside an otherwise read-only answer.
contribution: Provides a deterministic question-to-source router and a fail-soft answer composer that keeps brain responses read-only (proposals only) while reusing existing session and model primitives; it contributes the source-resolution primitive and the safe floor for brain answers.
summary_for_embedding: A Python backend module that routes operator questions to one of three cognition sources (fleet, recall, model) and composes grounded, read-only answers with fail-soft degrade to a conversational model.
intention_for_embedding: To implement the supervisor-as-loadable-brain backend: a safe, source-resolving mind that answers questions using existing fabric reads and model chat without autonomous action.

symbols.does: Deterministically maps a question string to a cognition source ('fleet', 'recall', or 'model') using keyword hints.
symbols.description: Implements the discrete source-selection primitive by lowercasing the question and checking two hint tuples. Fleet hints take precedence over recall hints; anything else falls back to the conversational 'model' source.

symbols.does: Reads recent channel posts from the store as grounded context for brain answers.
symbols.description: Composes existing channel event reads and content resolution to return the newest N channel posts oldest-to-newest. It is read-only and never posts; the post half remains gated elsewhere.

symbols.does: Composes the live-fabric answer from channel/session counts and recent traffic, optionally proposing gated actions.
symbols.description: Uses fold_channels and list_agent_sessions to summarize the fabric, folds recent channel traffic into a model turn when available, and surfaces wake/consult-like verbs as a gated proposal object rather than executing them.

symbols.does: Composes a conversational answer via Suite.chat using dynamic model selection and optional aim-grounding.
symbols.description: Picks a model based on an estimated context size, folds territory prose for an 'aim' into the prompt, calls suite.chat through the local Ollama host, and extracts the answer from the reply/text/answer fields.

symbols.does: Public entry point that resolves the source and returns a composed answer, degrading to model on errors.
symbols.description: Calls route_source, dispatches to the appropriate source composer, and catches exceptions to fall back to the 'model' source with a degrade note; if model also fails it returns an error dict.
